commit.message:
fix: Nix Build compatibility for Rust 1.93 nightly clippy + Quality Gate (#516)

Comprehensive fix for all nightly Rust 1.93 clippy lints across the workspace:

- Import ordering (nightly rustfmt)
- cloned_ref_to_slice_refs
- absurd_extreme_comparisons / assertions_on_constants
- single_match, collapsible_if, overly_complex_bool_expr
- needless_borrows_for_generic_args, needless_range_loop
- for_kv_map, dead_code, trailing whitespace, unused imports
- manual_range_contains, useless_vec, redundant_closure
- manual_checked_ops, unnecessary_sort_by

Also excludes tokmd-python from Quality Gate workspace checks (PyO3 unavailable in CI).

All CI jobs green: MSRV, Build+Test (ubuntu/windows), Deny, Feature Boundaries, Mutation Testing, Proptest, Nix Build, Docs, Publish Plan, Typos.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Parallelize build.py with multiprocessing.Pool(nproc) (#138)

* Initial plan

* Make build.py parallel with multiprocessing.Pool(nproc)

Use multiprocessing.Pool with os.cpu_count() workers to render posts,
collections, and pages in parallel. Each worker initializes its own
Liquid environment once via _init_worker, then _render_one handles
rendering and writing individual content items.

Benchmark (4 cores, 146 content items):
  Sequential: ~2.93s wall-clock
  Parallel:   ~1.98s wall-clock (1.48x speedup)

Co-authored-by: tekknolagi <401167+tekknolagi@users.noreply.github.com>

* Merge posts+collections into single parallel batch; drop unused collection .content writeback

Posts and collections had no interdependencies — the 3-batch sequential
ordering was happenstance. The only real dependency: feed.xml (a page)
reads post.content, so posts must finish before pages.

Profiled overhead (4 cores, 144 tasks):
  Pickling all tasks: ~23ms
  Pool creation+init: ~40ms
  Total overhead: ~63ms (2% of ~2900ms sequential build)

Benchmarks (median of 5, 4 cores):
  Sequential:          2.93s
  3-batch parallel:    2.01s
  2-batch parallel:    1.85s

Co-authored-by: tekknolagi <401167+tekknolagi@users.noreply.github.com>

---------

Co-authored-by: copilot-swe-agent[bot] <198982749+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
Co-authored-by: tekknolagi <401167+tekknolagi@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
test(engine): add deep watchdog and safety interlock tests (#178)

Add 57 comprehensive tests for watchdog and safety interlock subsystems:

- Concurrent SharedWatchdog access (thread-safe feed, arm/disarm, timeout visibility)
- Watchdog behavior during recovery sequences (timeout during fault, reset/rearm)
- Rapid state oscillation (fault/clear cycles, challenge/cancel cycles, estop/reset)
- Simultaneous/cascading faults (multi-fault logging, fault during challenge/ack)
- Timing guarantees (fault-to-zero <10ms, timeout response <1ms, estop <1ms)
- Challenge-response edge cases (wrong tokens, missing combo start, short hold, expiry)
- Safety state machine invariants (torque limits per state, hands-off timeout)
- Communication loss edge cases (no initial report, refresh prevents loss)
- Torque limit edge cases (zero max, safe mode limit)
- WatchdogTimeoutHandler edge cases (double timeout, reset/reuse)
- Property-based tests with proptest (200 cases per property):
  - Any fault yields zero torque
  - Early clear_fault rejected
  - clear_fault restores SafeTorque
  - Faulted blocks high-torque request
  - Watchdog timeout produces zero torque and fault
  - Torque bounded in normal operation
  - Emergency stop always zero
  - Clamp idempotent
  - Cancel challenge from SafeTorque fails

All tests return Result (no unwrap/expect per project policy).

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix(node): fix three bugs in Peers#cullExpiredNodesAndAddresses (#3346)

* fix(node): fix three bugs in Peers#cullExpiredNodesAndAddresses

Three bugs in the `#cullExpiredNodesAndAddresses` method of `Peers.ts`
prevented expired/orphaned peer nodes and stale addresses from ever being
cleaned up:

**Bug 1 – Early `return` when a commissioned node has exactly one address**
Inside the `for (const node of this)` loop, the shortcut path for
commissioned nodes with a single address used `return` instead of
`continue`.  A single such node anywhere in the peers map would
immediately exit the *entire* method, skipping all remaining peers.
This meant uncommissioned nodes that appeared after the first
single-address commissioned node in insertion order were never culled,
even after their TTL expired.

**Bug 2 – Early `return` in the "keep last address" branch**
The same `return`-instead-of-`continue` mistake in the branch that
preserves the freshest address when all addresses of a commissioned node
have expired.  Any commissioned node triggering this branch would silently
abort the loop and leave subsequent nodes un-processed.

**Bug 3 – Address update wrote back the original list**
After filtering expired addresses into `newAddresses`, the update call
passed `{ addresses }` (the *original* array captured at the top of the
loop body) instead of `{ addresses: newAddresses }`.  The result was a
no-op: the node's address list was never pruned.

Tests added in `ClientNodeTest.ts` ("orphaned peer cleanup" describe block):
- Verifies that an uncommissioned peer from an incomplete commissioning
  attempt persists across a controller restart (intentional: TTL hasn't
  expired yet, so the peer must survive for a potential retry).
- Verifies that an uncommissioned peer IS culled after its TTL expires
  even when a commissioned peer with one address is present (Bug 1/2
  regression test).
- Verifies that a stale address on a commissioned node is pruned on the
  next expiration tick (Bug 3 regression test).

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* formatting

---------

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

perf: replace Get-Az* API calls with pre-fetched data in Management.ps1

Pre-fetch in Initialize.ps1:
- Workspaces (Log Analytics), KeyVaultKeys, KeyVaultCertificates
- KeyVaults now fetched with full details (SoftDelete, PurgeProtection)

Replace in Management.ps1 (7 functions):
- F01.01: Get-AzOperationalInsightsWorkspace + Get-AzRoleAssignment
- F01.02: Get-AzOperationalInsightsWorkspace
- F01.03: Get-AzOperationalInsightsWorkspace + Get-AzStorageAccount
- F01.08: Get-AzSqlServer, Get-AzStorageAccount, Get-AzKeyVault
- F01.09: Get-AzKeyVaultKey
- F01.13: Get-AzOperationalInsightsWorkspace

All now use \ cached objects instead of direct API calls,
preventing token expiry errors during long assessment runs.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

security: harden against path traversal, temp file prediction, TOCTOU, and DoS

Patches:
- disk_io: Replace predictable pid^nanos^attempt XOR temp-file naming with
  FNV-1a hash mixing PID, timestamp, thread ID, monotonic counter, and
  stack address (ASLR entropy). Prevents temp-file prediction attacks.
- disk_io: Replace exists() TOCTOU in next_merge_sidecar_path with
  OpenOptions::create_new() for atomic check-and-create.
- render: Block path-traversal in resolve_image_url() by rejecting URLs
  containing '..' path components (both / and \ separators).
- parse: Add MAX_PARSE_BYTES (64 MiB) input size limit to prevent OOM
  from enormous markdown documents. Truncates at last newline boundary.
- height: Clamp font_size to 1000px and wrap_width to 100K to prevent
  f32 overflow producing Infinity in height estimation.

Fuzz/adversarial tests added:
- Parser: null bytes, control chars, deep nesting, long lines, unclosed
  markers, enormous tables, oversized input truncation, mixed constructs.
- Renderer: path traversal URLs, safe URL resolution, adversarial URLs,
  dot-dot segment detection, extreme height estimation values.
- Formatter: adversarial inputs, CRLF multibyte preservation, fence edge
  cases.
- Merger: empty/binary/CRLF/unicode inputs, pre-existing conflict markers.
- Disk I/O: temp file suffix uniqueness, atomic sidecar creation, special
  filenames.

360 tests pass, clippy clean, fmt clean.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Add end-to-end integration test suite (22 tests)

Tests cover all major CLI commands against a real Zabbix instance:

TestValidate (5 tests):
  - valid configs pass, invalid YAML fails, empty template name fails
  - bad interval fails, rich template passes

TestPlan (4 tests):
  - new template shows additions, after apply shows no changes
  - modification shows modify, rich template shows all resources

TestApply (5 tests):
  - creates template, is idempotent, updates item interval
  - rich template (dependent/calculated items + LLD), dry-run flag

TestDiff (3 tests):
  - no diff after apply, shows additions before apply
  - detects interval change

TestExport (3 tests):
  - produces valid YAML, unknown template fails
  - rich template preserves master_item_key / params / filter

TestFullRestore (2 tests):
  - minimal: export→delete→apply→plan(no changes)
  - rich: full round-trip verifying all item types, prototypes,
    dependent rules, calculated formulas in Zabbix API

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
chore(security): add CODEOWNERS and enable branch protection (#107)

## Summary

Mitigates two HIGH-severity OpenSSF Scorecard alerts from #103:

### Branch Protection (#17)
Enabled on `main` via GitHub API:
- Required status checks: **Lint**, **Coverage**
- Block force pushes and branch deletion
- No mandatory PR reviews (solo developer)

### Code Review (#30)
Added `.github/CODEOWNERS` assigning `@dorlugasigal` as default owner
for all files.

### Not addressed (by design)
| Alert | Severity | Reason |
|-------|----------|--------|
| Fuzzing (#34) | MEDIUM | Overkill for project scope |
| SAST (#33) | MEDIUM | Already at 81% coverage |
| CII Badge (#31) | LOW | Process step, not a code fix |
| Maintained (#29) | HIGH | Resolves with time |

Closes #103

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix(code_write): explicit workspace path instructions + better error messages

- Phase prompt now explicitly instructs agents to use absolute paths
  prefixed with WORKSPACE for code_write (not just cwd)
- Error message now lists allowed roots so agent can self-correct
- Fixes 'outside allowed workspace' loop on DK, CARE360 and others

Root cause: prompt said 'use for cwd' but code_write needs full absolute
path. Agents invented paths like /home/sfadmin/workspace/<name>/ which
are not in _ALLOWED_WRITE_ROOTS (/home/sfadmin/data/workspaces/<id>/).

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Add page-specific OG image templates for social previews

- Enhance /app/og/route.tsx with support for multiple page types (blog, work, about, resume, default)
- Add blog template: displays title, date, description, and author info
- Add work template: displays project title and description
- Add about template: displays profile picture with name and role
- Add resume template: displays name, role, and location
- Update all page metadata to use type-specific OG image generation:
  - Blog pages: type=blog with title, description, and publication date
  - Work pages: type=work with project title and summary
  - About page: type=about with minimal info
  - Resume page: type=resume with header info
- Add alt text to all images in OG generator for accessibility
- All pages now generate visually distinct link previews

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix(seal): respect [[seal.override]] fields from config when sealing

cmd_seal was only reading --fields from the CLI flag, ignoring any
[[seal.override]] entries in .gitvault/config.toml. This caused
`gitvault seal <file>` to encrypt all string values even when the
config restricted sealing to specific fields.

Fix: after computing the repo-relative path, look up matching
[[seal.override]] entries from config and use them as the field list
when no --fields CLI flag is provided. CLI --fields takes precedence.

Same fix applied to cmd_unseal for consistency.

Added regression test: test_cmd_seal_respects_config_override_fields

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
feat: implement P0 critical interpreter opcodes (closes #282) (#321)

Implement 13 high-priority opcodes that were hitting the catch-all
'unimplemented opcode' arm in the interpreter dispatch loop:

- DeletePropertySloppy / DeletePropertyStrict: delete named properties
- CreateRestParameter: collect surplus args into a rest array
- CreateMappedArguments / CreateUnmappedArguments: build arguments objects
- ThrowReferenceErrorIfHole: TDZ check for let/const
- ThrowSuperNotCalledIfHole / ThrowSuperAlreadyCalledIfNotHole: super() guards
- CallProperty0 / CallProperty1 / CallProperty2: fixed-arity property calls
- CallRuntime: stub for internal runtime dispatch
- StaNamedOwnProperty: own property definition
- StaLookupSlot: scope-chain variable store

Also fixes a pre-existing unused-import clippy warning by gating
MaglevJitCodeCache and TurbofanJitCodeCache imports behind cfg.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

calls would block on incomplete tasks. Use IsCompletedSuccessfully guard
to safely return null for unfinished tasks (handled by existing fallbacks).

commit.message:
squad: M1 Wave 2 — verification & IEEE spec sync

Milestone 1 Wave 2 completion:

ORCHESTRATION LOGS (NEW):
- 2026-03-05T15-50-m1-wave2-dale.md: Cross-implementation verification of M1
  features (extended booleans, raw text, ISO 8601 dates, duplicate keys)
  Result: 384 tests pass across all 3 implementations
  Issues: D1 (YYYY-MM dates missing in Python/JS), D2 (nested duplicate key
  structure differs between parsers)

- 2026-03-05T15-50-m1-wave2-rick.md: IEEE Spec synchronization from v0.1 to
  v0.2, adding 7 new sections and updating 7 existing sections to match
  TAML-SPEC.md v0.2.1. Document growth: 31KB → 58KB (+87%).

SESSION LOG (NEW):
- 2026-03-05T15-50-milestone1-wave2.md: Complete Wave 2 summary with test
  results, cross-implementation consistency matrix, critical issues D1 and D2,
  decision gate requirements, and recommendations for v0.3 release.

DECISIONS (UPDATED):
- Merged inbox files into decisions.md:
  + Milestone 1 Verification Report (Dale) — 384 tests, 2 issues identified
  + IEEE Spec Sync to v0.2 (Rick) — 7 new sections, 7 updated sections
- Deleted inbox files (deduplication):
  - dale-m1-verification.md
  - rick-ieee-sync.md

Key Findings:
- Extended booleans: Fully consistent across all 3 parsers ✅
- Raw text blocks: Fully consistent across all 3 parsers ✅
- ISO 8601 dates: Minor gap (YYYY-MM detection) in Python/JS ⚠️
- Duplicate bare keys: Critical structural difference between parsers ❌

Release Status: HOLD pending D2 decision (nested duplicate keys behavior).
D1 is medium-priority quick fix (1-2 hours per parser).

Add comprehensive telemetry session tests (#177)

* Add comprehensive telemetry session tests

Add 53 new tests covering telemetry session recording, replay,
and analysis in the telemetry-recorder crate:

- Session lifecycle: start/stop, restart, frames-before-start
- Recording format: JSON save/load roundtrip, flags preservation
- Replay fidelity: frame order, data match, progress tracking, reset
- Session metadata: game ID, car/track, duration, timestamps
- Multiple simultaneous sessions: independent recorders and players
- Large session handling: 5K-10K frame sessions with roundtrip
- Export formats: CSV, compact JSON, and binary (length-prefixed)
- Session comparison/diff: identical, metadata, frame data, symmetry
- Cross-format roundtrips: JSON↔binary equivalence
- Fixture scenarios: all TestScenario variants verified

New library features added to TelemetryRecording:
- to_csv(): Export as CSV string
- to_compact_json(): Export as compact JSON bytes
- to_binary()/from_binary(): Length-prefixed binary format
- diff(): Compare two recordings returning SessionDiff

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: replace deprecated .sequence field access with frame_index

The regression-prevention CI check flags .sequence as a deprecated
TelemetryData field. Replace f.sequence in CSV export with an
enumerate index (frame_index) and update tests accordingly.

commit.message:
feat(auth): Twitch Client ID を空欄OKに変更・アプリ同梱デフォルト対応

- src/auth.py: load_dotenv() を追加、APP_DEFAULT_CLIENT_ID定数追加
  get_effective_client_id() でユーザー設定→アプリ同梱の順にフォールバック
- src/gui.py: Client IDフィールドの説明文を「空欄OK・省略可」に変更
  start_auth / start_bot / _validate_channel_bg で get_effective_client_id() を使用

.env の TWITCH_CLIENT_ID にアプリ登録したClient IDを設定しておくことで
ユーザーはClient IDを取得・入力せずにそのままログインできるようになる。
ユーザーが自分のClient IDを入力した場合はそちらが優先される。

commit.message:
Parallelize Phase 2 startup tasks and remove arbitrary delays

- Remove Task.Delay() calls from CacheWarming (500ms), MachineLearning (2000ms), and DynamicConfig (1500ms)
- Replace sequential foreach with Task.WhenAll() so all 4 init tasks run in parallel
- Preserve per-task try-catch for independent failure handling

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Add exception safety to fire-and-forget event invocations in PaperTradingService

Wrap 5 event invocations (TradeClosed x2, TradeOpened x1, PriceUpdated x2)
in try-catch blocks to prevent subscriber exceptions from crashing the
service or breaking trade flow. Exceptions are logged as warnings via _logger.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Parallelize asset data loading in SmartTrainingService

Replace sequential foreach loop with bounded parallel loading using
SemaphoreSlim(4) and Task.WhenAll. Use ConcurrentBag for thread-safe
result collection. Independent I/O operations now run concurrently
with max 4 concurrent loads.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Add 30s timeout to ProphecyEngine Phase 2 Task.WhenAll

Wrap the Phase 2 parallel analysis Task.WhenAll with a linked
CancellationTokenSource and WaitAsync to prevent indefinite hangs
if a service deadlocks. On timeout, logs a warning and adds a
partial-results notice to prophecy.Warnings.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

feat: initial Northwind DevOps Standards repository with reusable workflows and composite actions

Includes:
- 4 reusable workflows: ci-dotnet, ci-node, docker-publish, security-scan
- 3 composite actions: setup-dotnet-env, setup-node-env, publish-test-results
- Documentation: CI/CD standards, testing strategy, adoption guide
- Example caller workflows for .NET, Node.js, and Docker
- CODEOWNERS, dependabot config, and MIT license

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix: use /api/group/pause whenever group_id is set (#50)

* fix: use /api/group/pause whenever group_id is set, not just groupSize > 1

Previously onDevicePause() fell back to /api/pause when the locally
visible group had only 1 member (e.g. after MA restructured groups
post-pause). If group_id is non-null the player is in an MA group and
/api/group/pause must always be used — MA owns group membership.

commit.message:
feat: global functions + iterator helpers (closes #300) (#320)

Add ES2025 Iterator prototype helpers (map, filter, take, drop, flatMap,
reduce, toArray, forEach, some, every, find) and Iterator.from static
method to iterator.rs. Wire Iterator constructor and globalThis property
in install_globals.rs. Fix pre-existing unused import warning in
interpreter/mod.rs by gating platform-specific JIT cache types behind
cfg(all(target_arch = "x86_64", unix)).
